01_initial_request.input_data: Experimental protein-protein interaction (PPI) data, quantitative proteomics data across life stages, and AlphaFold-Multimer structural models
01_initial_request.user_intent: To prioritize novel drug targets within the editosome to accelerate antiparasitic drug discovery and bypass the need for prior functional characterization
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Quantitative ranking of top hub editosome proteins and identification of key interaction interfaces and hotspot residues

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Trypanosoma brucei editosome contains approximately 70 proteins involved in RNA editing. [Gap] Need to reconstruct the protein-protein interaction network to identify hub proteins. [Decision] To achieve network reconstruction, chose to use TrypsNetDB to retrieve experimentally substantiated interactome data for 74 mitochondrial RNA processing proteins, expecting to obtain interaction data with experimental validation scores.

parameters.protein_count: 74 proteins associated with mitochondrial RNA processing

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Retrieved interaction data with assay scores reflecting level of support from number of experimental techniques confirming validity

references.citation: Gazestani VH, Yip CW, Nikpour N, Berghuis N, Salavati R, TrypsNetDB. PLoS Negl Trop Dis. 2017
references.relevance: Provides comprehensive database of experimentally substantiated interactome data specific to trypanosomatid proteins

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Interaction data has been retrieved from TrypsNetDB. [Gap] Need to visualize the network for analysis. [Decision] To achieve network visualization, chose to use Cytoscape to visualize final interaction data, expecting to obtain visual representation of 90 nodes with 677 edges.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Visualized editosome PPI network with proteins as nodes and experimentally verified interactions as edges

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Network has been visualized in Cytoscape. [Gap] Need to identify hub proteins through topological analysis. [Decision] To achieve hub identification, chose to use CytoNCA plugin to perform centrality analysis calculating degree, betweenness and closeness measures, expecting to obtain centrality scores for each protein.

parameters.edge_weights: assay scores assigned as weights during centrality analysis

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Calculated three centrality measures known to best represent hub nodes in biological networks

references.citation: Tang Y, Li M, Wang J, Pan Y, Wu F-X. CytoNCA. BioSystems. 2015
references.relevance: Provides Cytoscape plugin for centrality analysis and evaluation of protein interaction networks

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Centrality measures have been calculated for all proteins. [Gap] Need to rank proteins using multiple criteria to identify true hubs. [Decision] To achieve protein ranking, chose to use Multi-criteria decision analysis (MCDA) with equal weightage to three centralities, expecting to obtain MCDA scores ranging from 0 to 1 for each protein.

parameters.centrality_weightage: equal weightage given to three centralities
parameters.hub_threshold: MCDA score of more than 0.7 were considered as hubs

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 10 proteins selected as top-rank hub proteins with MCDA >= 0.6, predominantly RESC proteins comprising 85% of all known RESC proteins

references.citation: Mesgari I, Kermani MAMA, Hanneman R, Aliahmadi A. Mathematical Technology of networks. 2015
references.relevance: Provides methodology for identifying key nodes using multi-criteria decision-making tools

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Top hub proteins have been identified with MCDA scores. [Gap] Need to model 3D structures of hub PPIs for interface analysis. [Decision] To achieve structure prediction, chose to use AlphaFold_2.0_multimer.v3 to model binary PPIs involving hub proteins, expecting to obtain 3D interaction structures.

parameters.filtering_criteria: supported by at least one RNase positive experimental technique

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 22 hub PPIs identified, 20 structures successfully modeled, 6 hub PPIs with very low average PAEs at interfaces after filtering

references.citation: Evans R, O'Neill M, Pritzel A, et al. Protein complex prediction with AlphaFold-Multimer. Biorxiv. 2021
references.relevance: Provides AlphaFold-Multimer for accurate structural resolution of protein interactions

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] AlphaFold-Multimer models have been generated. [Gap] Need to optimize amino acid side chain positions for accuracy. [Decision] To achieve structure optimization, chose to use AMBER molecular dynamics simulation to optimize amino acid side chain position of top-ranked models, expecting to obtain refined structures.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Optimized amino acid side chain positions of top-ranked AlphaFold-Multimer models

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PPI structures have been modeled and optimized. [Gap] Need to visualize and analyze structures for interface identification. [Decision] To achieve structure visualization and analysis, chose to use UCSF ChimeraX to visualize PPI structures and select interface residues, expecting to obtain visual representation with interface residue identification.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Selected residues from interacting proteins within ≤5 Angstroms as primary interface residues, filtered based on PAE values

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Interface residues have been identified from structural models. [Gap] Need to analyze residue-level interactions for hotspot identification. [Decision] To achieve residue interaction network analysis, chose to use RING 3.0 to generate RINs based on bound-state structure of hub proteins, expecting to obtain graphical representation with residues as nodes and physicochemical interactions as edges.

parameters.structure_type: bound-state structure of hub protein and interaction partners

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated RINs as graphical representation of protein structures with residues as nodes and physicochemical interactions as edges

references.citation: Clementel D, Del Conte A, Monzon AM, et al. RING 3.0. Nucleic Acids Res. 2022
references.relevance: Provides fast generation of probabilistic residue interaction networks from structural ensembles

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] RINs have been generated for hub proteins. [Gap] Need to identify central residues and hotspot residues within interfaces. [Decision] To achieve hotspot identification, chose to perform centrality analysis on reconstructed weighted RINs, expecting to obtain centrality measures for each residue.

parameters.hotspot_criteria: central residues coinciding with confirmed interface

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified central residues within proteins; those coinciding with confirmed interface categorized as hotspot residues

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hotspot residues have been identified through RIN analysis. [Gap] Need to rank hotspot residues quantitatively. [Decision] To achieve residue ranking, chose to use dual-criteria approach considering RIN-based MCDA score and PAE values, expecting to obtain ranked hotspot residues.

parameters.criteria: RIN-based MCDA score and PAE values

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Ranked identified hotspot residues, found hotspots mostly enriched with tyrosine and arginine residues

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Computational analysis of PPIs and hotspots completed. [Gap] Need to validate findings with experimental proteomics data across life stages. [Decision] To achieve data preprocessing, chose to use missForest R package to impute missing values in protein abundances, expecting to obtain complete proteomics dataset.

parameters.timepoints: different developmental stages of T. brucei TREU927

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Imputed missing values in protein abundances from quantitative proteomics data

references.citation: Stekhoven DJ, Bühlmann P. MissForest. Bioinformatics. 2012
references.relevance: Provides non-parametric missing value imputation for mixed-type data

references.citation: Dejung M, Subota I, Bucerius F, et al. PLoS Pathog. 2016
references.relevance: Source of quantitative proteomics data from different developmental stages

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteomics data has been preprocessed with imputed values. [Gap] Need to identify differentially expressed proteins across life stages. [Decision] To achieve differential expression analysis, chose to use DEqMS to perform DEA accounting for variance-peptide relationship, expecting to obtain fold changes and adjusted p-values.

parameters.input: number of detected peptides for each protein

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained fold changes and adjusted p-values from DEA, all detected hub proteins except KREL1 showed differential expression during LS to SS differentiation

references.citation: Zhu Y, Orre LM, Tran YZ, et al. DEqMS. Mol Cell Proteomics. 2020
references.relevance: Provides method for accurate variance estimation in differential protein expression analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Differential expression analysis completed. [Gap] Need to perform gene set analysis for biological context. [Decision] To achieve gene set analysis, chose to use piano package to perform GSA using GO term dataset, expecting to obtain significantly enriched GO terms.

parameters.GO_terms: 35,448 GO terms from T. brucei GO dataset

02_agent_trajectory.observation: GSA showed 35 GO terms significantly downregulated in SS form, identified significant terms related to mitochondrial RNA editing

references.citation: Zhu Y, Orre LM, Tran YZ, et al. DEqMS. Mol Cell Proteomics. 2020

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Gene set analysis completed showing RNA editing terms enriched. [Gap] Need to validate PPIs through co-expression analysis across developmental stages. [Decision] To achieve PPI validation, chose to perform co-expression analysis assessing correlation in expression patterns between protein pairs, expecting to obtain correlation coefficients across timepoints.

parameters.timepoints: different developmental stages from LS to SS to PF

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Most hub PPIs exhibited positive correlation; KREL2-KREPA1 showed negative correlation overall but positive during 6 to 48h; RESC12A-RESC13 significantly correlated throughout developmental stages

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Cross-validation through proteomics data analysis (differential expression analysis and gene set analysis) and co-expression analysis across developmental stages

hub_protein_MCDA_threshold.interpretation: 10 top-rank hub proteins selected with MCDA >= 0.6, predominantly RESC proteins

PPI_interface_PAE_threshold.interpretation: 6 hub PPIs with very low average PAEs at interfaces after filtering models with PAE > 10 Å

interface_distance_cutoff.interpretation: Residues within ≤5 Å selected as primary interface residues

RIN_MCDA_cutoff.interpretation: Hotspot residues ranked using RIN-based MCDA score cutoff of 0.5

GSA_significance_threshold.interpretation: 35 GO terms significantly downregulated in SS form, including mitochondrial RNA editing terms

differential_expression_threshold.interpretation: All detected hub proteins except KREL1 showed differential expression during LS to SS differentiation

top_PPIs_identified.interpretation: KREL2-KREPA1, RESC2-RESC1, RESC10-RESC6, KREL1-KREPA2, RESC12A-RESC13, RESC3-RESC2 accurately modeled

RESC2_RESC1_RMSD.interpretation: AlphaFold model aligns closely with cryo-EM structure, validating modeling accuracy

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The study successfully quantitatively ranked top hub editosome proteins (10 proteins with MCDA >= 0.6), identified key interaction interfaces (6 accurately modeled PPIs), and identified hotspot residues (enriched with tyrosine and arginine). Findings were cross-validated through proteomics data showing differential expression of hub proteins and co-expression correlation of PPIs across developmental stages. Top candidates highlighted include KREL2-KREPA1, RESC2-RESC1, RESC12A-RESC13, and RESC10-RESC6 for further drug target investigation.
